page: Amazon Web Services t2.micro is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t2 server family. It features 1 vCPU, 1 physical core, and 1 thread per core, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU architecture is i386. It is equipped with 1.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 30 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it operates on the Xen hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the t2.micro in the lower tier of cloud resources. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests both score 157.0, categorizing it in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show 78.35 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 10.85 GB/sec for large uncached read blocks and 7.0 GB/sec for large uncached write blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression achieves 20.05 GB/sec, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 7.26 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores are 1438.31 and 755.64 respectively, reflecting its entry-level processing capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2.micro offers a low-resource entry point suitable for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that require burst capacity. It is designed for budget-conscious scenarios where continuous high performance is not required. The instance is suited for lightweight applications such as static web serving, which benchmarks at 1.12 GB/sec, or low-volume Redis key-value stores, which handle 0.51 million SET operations per second. It is also appropriate for development environments, basic testing, and microservices with low traffic.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.micro is a burstable, entry-level instance within the t2 family, running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor with an i386 architecture, offering 1 vCPU, 1 physical core, and 1.0 GB of system memory. The instance has no local storage or GPU resources, and its baseline network bandwidth is 0 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng CPU scores of 157.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 1438.31, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth ranges from 78.35 GB/sec for small cached blocks to 10.85 GB/sec for large uncached reads. Given its burstable CPU allocation and low resource density, the t2.micro is a highly cost-efficient option for low-traffic workloads, such as static web serving, basic software testing, and lightweight development environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.micro is a burstable t2 instance featuring 1 vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor. It is designed for entry-level, low-traffic workloads and basic testing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t2.micro is a burstable instance with 1 vCPU, 1.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for low-traffic and development workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable single-core instance with one gigabyte of memory, designed for low-intensity development and basic testing workloads.

1.0 GB of system memory with a burstable CPU allocation model
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Xen hypervisor virtualization with 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for static web serving, microservices, and development environments
